rationale: Low spatial resolution and central differences are not ideal for advection; explicit RK4 is stable at this CFL but accuracy is limited by grid and scheme. Efficiency is good, but overall suitability is moderate.

High spatial and temporal resolution with 4th order upwind FD and SSPRK(3,3) is well-suited for advection, providing good accuracy and stability. Computational cost is high but justified for accuracy.
Medium resolution FV with upwind flux and RK2 is robust and efficient for advection, though accuracy is limited to second order. Good balance of cost and reliability.

Implicit Crank-Nicolson is unnecessary for non-stiff advection and can introduce numerical diffusion; FEM is flexible but less efficient and accurate than upwind or spectral methods for this periodic, smooth problem.

Spectral Fourier with periodic BCs and explicit RK4 is nearly optimal for smooth advection, offering spectral accuracy and excellent stability at low CFL. High computational cost is justified by accuracy.
Implicit backward Euler is overly diffusive and low order for pure advection, leading to significant accuracy loss. Stability is high but not needed here; efficiency is moderate.
IMEX is unnecessary for non-stiff advection; FEM is flexible but less efficient and accurate than upwind or spectral for this problem. Moderate resolution and order.

High-res FD with WENO5 and SSPRK(3,3) is highly robust and accurate for advection, especially with non-smooth solutions, though here the solution is smooth. Slightly less efficient than spectral but very suitable.
Spectral Fourier with IMEX is highly accurate and robust, but IMEX is not needed for pure advection. Slightly less efficient than explicit RK, but still excellent for this problem.
Lax-Friedrichs is highly diffusive and only first order, leading to poor accuracy for smooth advection. Stability is good but at the cost of significant numerical dissipation.
